name: TerraGrip WeatherRun
description: Stable running shoe with a grippy outsole, reinforced heel, and weather-resistant upper. Designed for runners who want strong foot support during wet commutes and rainy-season training.

name: CloudSprint Daily
description: Lightweight road running shoe with soft cushioning for daily miles. Feels fast and breathable, but it is not ideal for heavy rain or muddy surfaces.

name: TrailGuard Peak
description: Trail running shoe built for uneven paths, wet grass, and slippery surfaces. The shoe offers a stable platform and extra arch support for longer outdoor sessions.

name: CityStride Knit
description: Casual trainer for walking and light gym use. Comfortable for everyday wear, but it lacks the structure and weather protection needed for serious running.

name: StormFlex Support
description: Supportive running shoe made for runners who want stability, a secure midfoot feel, and dependable grip in wet urban conditions. Good option for rainy weather and overpronation support.

name: FeatherFlow Racer
description: Very light neutral running shoe built for speed and short road sessions. Breathable and flexible, but not intended for strong support or bad weather.

name: WildPath Shield
description: Trail-focused shoe with sticky rubber traction, protective toe bumper, and coated upper for wet terrain. Useful for runners looking for grip and structure on rainy trail routes.

name: CalmMove Everyday
description: Comfort-first lifestyle sneaker with soft foam and relaxed fit. Great for errands and casual outfits, but not recommended for technical running or support-heavy needs.

name: Free From Protein Oats
description: Creamy high-protein oat pot for quick breakfasts. Made without dairy ingredients and suitable for gluten-sensitive customers who want a filling start to the day.

name: Berry Boost Granola
description: Crunchy berry granola with honey and oat clusters. Popular for breakfast bowls, but it contains gluten and is not suitable for strict vegan diets.

name: Plant Kitchen Chia Pot
description: Ready-to-eat chia and coconut breakfast pot with fruit puree. Light, dairy-free, and convenient for vegan shoppers looking for a small breakfast option.

name: Wholegrain Wheat Biscuits
description: Classic cereal biscuits for a budget breakfast. High in fiber, but contains gluten and is usually paired with milk or yogurt.

name: Almond Protein Pancake Mix
description: Quick pancake mix with added protein and nutty flavor. Works for an easy weekend breakfast, but it contains milk powder and is not suitable for lactose intolerance.

name: Wellness Seed Porridge Cup
description: Single-serve porridge cup with flax, chia, and oats. A practical breakfast choice for vegan customers who want something light and easy at work.

name: Free From Cocoa Crunch
description: Chocolate-flavored breakfast cereal made without gluten or dairy ingredients. Designed for shoppers who need a free-from option without spending too much.

name: Recovery Protein Shake Vanilla
description: Ready-to-drink protein shake with whey protein and added vitamins. Useful after workouts, but it is dairy-based and not a breakfast-friendly vegan option.

name: Nut Butter Breakfast Bar
description: Portable breakfast bar with peanut butter, oats, and seeds. Good for busy mornings, but it contains gluten and is not suitable for strict dairy-free shoppers.

name: Green Blend Oat Smoothie
description: Chilled oat-based smoothie with spinach, banana, and pea protein. Marketed as a dairy-free breakfast drink for customers who want something quick and plant-based.

name: RainLine Stability
description: Everyday running shoe with supportive sidewalls, durable grip, and a treated upper that handles drizzle and slick pavements well. Built for runners who want structure without moving into trail shoes.

name: PulseRoad Flex
description: Responsive neutral shoe for shorter road runs and gym sessions. It feels agile and breathable, but it offers limited arch support and little protection in wet weather.

name: NorthTrack Utility
description: Hybrid outdoor trainer designed for mixed terrain, park routes, and damp morning runs. Delivers moderate support with extra outsole grip for slippery ground.

name: StudioLite Move
description: Minimal lightweight trainer aimed at studio classes, treadmill sessions, and short walks. Comfortable indoors, but not a strong match for support-heavy or rain-focused running needs.

name: MetroShield Runner
description: Road running shoe with firm heel support, wet-surface traction, and a weather-ready upper. Designed for commuters who want a stable feel under 100 pounds.

name: OpenStride Air
description: Highly breathable daily trainer focused on comfort and easy miles. Good for dry weather and light jogging, but it is not suitable for rainy conditions or strong stability needs.

name: SummitGrip Traverse
description: Protective off-road running shoe with aggressive lugs and extra midfoot structure. Suited to muddy trail sessions and runners who want both grip and support.

name: SoftPace Comfort
description: Plush everyday sneaker for errands, travel, and casual wear. Cushioned and easy to wear, but not engineered for performance running or structured support.

name: Coconut Quinoa Breakfast Pot
description: Ready-made breakfast pot with quinoa, coconut, and fruit compote. Suitable for vegan shoppers seeking a gluten-free option that feels slightly more premium.

name: Maple Oat Breakfast Muffins
description: Soft oat muffins designed for easy grab-and-go breakfasts. Dairy-free, but they contain wheat and are not suitable for gluten intolerance.

name: Greek Style Berry Yogurt Bowl
description: High-protein yogurt bowl with berry compote and crunchy topping. Popular for breakfast, but it contains dairy and is not vegan.

name: Seeded Banana Breakfast Loaf
description: Sliced breakfast loaf with banana, seeds, and oats. Marketed as a filling budget breakfast, but it contains gluten and may not fit strict dietary filters.

name: Vanilla Rice Protein Cereal
description: Light breakfast cereal made with rice crisps and pea protein. Free from dairy and gluten, and intended for shoppers who want a simple low-cost pantry option.

name: Morning Berry Oat Jar
description: Chilled overnight oats with berry puree and chia seeds. Designed for quick breakfast routines and suitable for dairy-free customers, though it contains gluten.

name: Protein Pancake Bites
description: Portable pancake bites with added protein for busy mornings. Convenient and filling, but they contain milk and are not suitable for vegan or lactose-free diets.

name: Golden Corn Crunch
description: Crunchy corn-based cereal aimed at free-from shoppers. It is gluten free and dairy free, but the honey coating means it is not vegan.

name: Pea Protein Breakfast Shake
description: Shelf-stable oat and pea protein shake positioned as a breakfast replacement. Plant-based, dairy-free, and easy to take on the go.

name: Cinnamon Bran Squares
description: Budget cereal with cinnamon flavor and added fiber. Works for cheap breakfasts, but it contains gluten and is not a strong fit for strict dietary filtering.
